title: Daily Briefing — Friday, May 22
tldr: A trojanized “Nx Console” VS Code extension harvested credentials across AI firms — ~3,800 internal repos stolen while the malicious build was live just ~18 minutes.
Cheap, fast multimodal piled up: Gemini 3.5 Flash GA ($1.50/$9 per 1M, 76.2% Terminal-Bench), a Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite preview, and NVIDIA's open Nemotron 3 Nano Omni (30B MoE, ~9× throughput).
Money kept moving: SAP to acquire Prior Labs (>$1.18B over 4 yrs), Legora's $550M Series D at $5.55B, and a new $300M BMW i Ventures agentic-AI fund.

blocks.sub: Supply-chain breach via a dev extension
blocks.p: A trojanized Nx Console VS Code extension harvested developer credentials across multiple AI firms — ~3,800 internal repos exfiltrated despite the malicious build being live only ~18 minutes.
blocks.why: Agentic dev tooling massively expands the attack surface; a single poisoned extension can drain an org.
blocks.doIt: Pin and verify IDE extensions, rotate tokens, and treat your editor as production infrastructure.

blocks.p: Gemini 3.5 Flash hit GA ($1.50/$9 per 1M tokens, ~1M context, 76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1, beating 3.1 Pro on coding/agents); Google previewed 3.1 Flash-Lite; NVIDIA released open-weight Nemotron 3 Nano Omni (30B MoE, ~9× throughput).
blocks.why: “Good enough, fast, cheap” is becoming the default production tier — reserve frontier models for the hard 10%.
blocks.doIt: Benchmark a Flash-class model on your highest-volume task this week.

blocks.p: SAP agreed to acquire Prior Labs (>$1.18B over four years) to build a structured-business-data lab; legal-AI Legora raised $550M (Series D) at $5.55B; BMW i Ventures launched a $300M fund for agentic, physical, and industrial AI.

sources: llm-stats.com · crescendo.ai · digitalapplied tracker · company announcements (figures directional where noted).
